title: Conophyllum clivorum
taxonomy: Conophyllum clivorum is a species of extinct conodonts, which are ancient marine animals known for their distinctive tooth-like structures. Conophyllum clivorum is classified within the clade Conodonta and belongs to the family Tribrachididae. This species is thought to have lived during the Early Ordovician period, approximately 480-470 million years ago. The classification of Conophyllum clivorum is based on its unique conodont elements, which provide valuable information about its evolutionary relationships. As a result, Conophyllum clivorum is an important fossil species for understanding the evolution of early vertebrates.

intro: Conophyllum clivorum is a flowering plant species in the family Orchidaceae, native to the subtropical regions of China. Conophyllum clivorum has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Conophyllum clivorum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
